prompt:
An assembled entry-level FDM 3D printer with a 0.4 mm nozzle sits on a workbench, its 180×180×180 mm build volume clearly framed by the gantry and bed. On the side, a 500 g spool of PLA filament is mounted on a simple holder, with the filament routed through a guide toward the extruder. The print bed is empty and clean, and the control panel shows a neutral standby screen, emphasizing that this is a ready-to-use kit with printer and filament included.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk maker corner with soft, even lighting, neatly organized tools and filament spools on shelves, and a plant or two adding warmth. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on the printer, and a composition that centers the printer frame, bed, and PLA spool while the rest of the workspace remains softly blurred, highlighting a compact starter 3D printing setup.
